metadata.source: Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, China Leather Industry Association, China Footwear Industry Association, National Bureau of Statistics

industry_overview.title: China Footwear Industry Overview (2026)
industry_overview.summary: China is the world's largest footwear producer and exporter, accounting for approximately 60% of global shoe production. The industry encompasses athletic shoes, leather shoes, casual shoes, women's shoes, children's shoes, and specialty footwear. In 2026, the industry is projected to exceed ¥820 billion in revenue, driven by athleisure trends, rising domestic consumption, export resilience, and technological advancements in materials and manufacturing. The industry is undergoing transformation toward automation, sustainable materials, and brand upgrading.

key_policy_initiatives.name: Action Plan for High-Quality Development of Leather and Footwear Industry (2024-2026)
key_measures: Promote eco-friendly materials (bio-based, recycled, water-based adhesives)
Support automation and digitalization in shoe manufacturing
Encourage brand building and international marketing
Establish national-level footwear design and innovation centers
Strengthen quality and safety standards (chemicals, durability)

current_status_2025.automation_rate: 45% (cutting and assembly lines in large factories)

subsectors: athletic shoes (running, basketball, training, casual sneakers)

applications: retail (shoe stores, department stores, brand outlets)
export to global markets (US, EU, Japan, Southeast Asia, Africa)
OEM/ODM for international brands (Nike, Adidas, Skechers, Clarks)

key_hubs.supplier_example: Anta, Xtep, 361°, Peak all headquartered here; thousands of suppliers, including large OEMs like Yue Yuen (factory)
Putian is known for high-quality shoe production, including for Nike and Adidas via OEMs

key_hubs.specialization: Women's fashion shoes, wholesale markets (站西路)
key_hubs.supplier_example: Guangzhou footwear wholesale markets – thousands of manufacturers and trading companies
Dongguan Xinghui Footwear – produces for European luxury brands
Aokang, Red Dragonfly headquartered here; also many OEMs
Chengdu Women's Shoes Industrial Park – many factories
Yangzhou shoe cluster – exports slippers worldwide

athletic_shoes.description: Running shoes, basketball shoes, training shoes, sneakers, hiking shoes. Largest segment driven by sports participation and athleisure.
athletic_shoes.market_trends: Knitted uppers; supercritical foams; sustainability (recycled materials); customization.

leather_shoes.description: Dress shoes, oxfords, loafers, boots, casual leather shoes. Strong in men's and women's segments.
leather_shoes.market_trends: Lightweight leather; comfort technology; Goodyear welt revival.

women's_shoes.description: High heels, pumps, sandals, boots, flats, wedges. Highly fashion-driven.
women's_shoes.market_trends: Comfort heels (block heels, platforms); sustainable materials; versatile designs.

children's_shoes.description: Infant, toddler, and youth shoes. Growing focus on foot health and comfort.
children's_shoes.market_trends: Lightweight; flexible soles; breathable materials; educational brands.

casual_shoes.description: Canvas shoes, slip-ons, boat shoes, espadrilles. Retro styles popular.
casual_shoes.market_trends: Sustainable canvas; recycled rubber; vintage designs.

sandals_slippers.description: Flip-flops, slides, beach sandals, house slippers. High volume, lower price point.
sandals_slippers.market_trends: EVA foam; ergonomic designs; sustainable materials.

safety_shoes.description: Steel-toe, composite-toe, anti-static, puncture-resistant, slip-resistant for industrial use.
safety_shoes.market_trends: Lightweight composites; comfort insoles; modern designs.

supplier_evaluation_metrics.target: ISO 9001, ISO 14001; specific certifications (SATRA, BLC for leather, SATRA for testing)
BSCI, SMETA, SA8000, or customer code of conduct audits
Cutting, stitching, lasting, bottoming lines; monthly output; specialized machines (CNC cutting, automatic stitching)
REACH, RoHS, CA Prop 65; leather sourcing ethical (LWG certification)
In-house design team, ability to develop samples, 3D modeling

verification_methods: Conduct factory audit (production processes, QC, working conditions)
Order samples for fit, comfort, and durability testing (flex, abrasion, adhesion)
Use third-party inspection (SGS, Bureau Veritas, TÜV) for pre-shipment
communication_best_practices: Provide detailed product specifications (last number, size range, materials, colors, construction details)
Share design sketches, 3D drawings, or reference samples
Discuss sample development timeline and cost
Clarify packaging requirements (inner box, outer carton, labeling)
Establish quality agreement (AQL levels, acceptable defects)
sourcing_guide.payment_terms: Typical: 30% deposit, 70% balance before shipment. For custom last/mold development, 50% deposit, 50% on sample approval.

lead_times.bulk_production: 45-75 days after sample approval (depending on order size)

faq.question: What are typical MOQs for shoes in China?
faq.answer: For athletic shoes, MOQ often 1000-3000 pairs per design/color. For leather shoes, 500-1500 pairs. For sandals/slippers, 2000-5000 pairs. Some factories accept lower MOQs (200-500) at higher prices or for sample orders.

faq.question: Do Chinese suppliers provide custom last making?
faq.answer: Yes, custom lasts are essential for proper fit. Last making costs $500-$2000 per size range. Provide detailed foot measurements or reference lasts.

faq.question: How can I ensure the quality of leather?
faq.answer: Request leather samples and specifications (thickness, grade, finish). Work with tanneries that have LWG (Leather Working Group) certification. Use third-party testing for chemical compliance.

faq.question: What are the most popular sole materials?
faq.answer: EVA for lightweight comfort (athletic, casual); rubber for durability (outdoor, work); PU for cushioning; TPR for affordability. Specify requirements.

faq.question: Can I get shoes with custom branding and packaging?
faq.answer: Yes, most suppliers offer OEM/ODM. They can emboss logos on insoles, hang tags, custom boxes. Minimum quantities apply.

glossary.EVA: Ethylene-vinyl acetate – lightweight foam used in midsoles.
glossary.PU: Polyurethane – durable, used in soles and synthetic leather.
glossary.TPR: Thermoplastic rubber – flexible, used in soles.
glossary.TPU: Thermoplastic polyurethane – durable, used in soles and overlays.
glossary.Last: Three-dimensional form used to shape shoes.
glossary.Vulcanization: Process that bonds rubber sole to upper using heat (e.g., canvas shoes).
glossary.Goodyear welt: Traditional construction for high-end leather shoes.
glossary.LWG: Leather Working Group – certification for sustainable leather production.
glossary.SATRA: UK-based research and testing organization for footwear.
